title: Fault-tolerant federated distributed database
abstract: The present disclosure discloses methods and systems for forming a fault-tolerant federated distributed database system (100) that includes a plurality of globally distributed local agreement groups (106) each including a plurality of computing nodes (104). The method includes storing (402) data in each computing node in accordance with a temporal data model (200). a block corresponding to the temporal data model storing keys (210) and time value (208) and data reference value (206) corresponding to each key. Then, for each globally distributed local agreement group, blocks of corresponding computing nodes are updated (404), by updating state of corresponding keys simultaneously using a local agreement algorithm. Thereafter, for each globally distributed local agreement group (106), updated blocks are combined (406) to form a combined block of key edits, and which are then being agreed (408) upon based on a pre-defined range of time and keys, using a global distributed agreement algorithm.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of forming a fault-tolerant federated distributed database system (100). wherein the federated distributed database system (100) includes a plurality of globally distributed local agreement groups (106). each globally distributed local agreement group including a plurality of computing nodes (104), the method comprising: storing (402) data in each computing node in accordance with a temporal data model, wherein a block corresponding to the temporal data model stores one or more keys, and time value and data reference value corresponding to each key, wherein the time value determines a point in time at which corresponding key is assigned corresponding data reference value; updating (404), for each globally distributed local agreement group, one or more blocks of corresponding computing nodes, by updating state of corresponding one or more keys simultaneously using a local agreement algorithm; combining (406). for each globally distributed local agreement group, one or more updated blocks of the one or more corresponding computing nodes to form a corresponding combined block of key edits; and globally agreeing (408) to one or more key edits of combined blocks of the plurality of globally distributed local agreement groups, based on a pre -defined range of time and keys, using a global distributed agreement algorithm. 2. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein the local agreement algorithm is PAXOS algorithm. 3. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein the global agreement algorithm is based on a blockchain distributed ledger technology. 4. The method as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the blockchain distributed ledger technology is Ripple protocol that is used to manage simultaneous updates of keys coming from one or more globally distributed domain groups, thereby offering global cross domain transactions at a delay of few seconds. 5. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein the time-dependent nature of data model of each computing node facilitates determining the consistent state of the fault -tolerant federated distributed database system at every time instant. 6. The method as claimed in claim 1 further comprising mapping each data reference with a data value of a block of data in a corresponding data store. 7. The method as claimed in claim 7, wherein the data store is a type of distributed NoSQL key-value store. 8. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein the data reference is fixed bit-length data, and the updates to one or more keys include updating corresponding fixed bit -length data references and corresponding time values. 9. The method as claimed in claim 8, wherein the data reference is a hash code of data bytes generated using a hashing algorithm on corresponding data, and the hashing algorithm is selected from at least one of: MD5, SHA-l, SHA-256, and SHA-384. 10. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein key updates of the first and second combined blocks are managed based on a pre -defined condition, wherein the pre-defined condition is to check if a key update is within the key-time range specified in a transaction request. 11. The method as claimed in claim 1 further comprising dynamically forming a local agreement group with a dynamic number of nodes and a dynamic key range determined in response to load and atomic transaction requirements. 12. The method as claimed in claim 1 further comprising storing one or more links of a growing chain against the same key in the temporal data model at different time instants, for blockchain based operations. 13. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein a key is updated during a transaction among the plurality of computing nodes, and a new time entry is appended to corresponding temporal data model, with a new data reference to a new data. 14. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein each node within a local agreement group has a similar pre defined key range for storing corresponding time values and data reference values. 15. A system (300) for forming a fault-tolerant federated distributed database system, wherein the federated distributed database system includes a plurality of globally distributed local agreement groups (106). each globally distributed local agreement group (106) including a plurality of computing nodes (104). the system comprising: a data storing module (302) for storing data in each computing node (106) in accordance with a temporal data model (200). a block corresponding to the temporal data model (200) storing one or more keys (210). and time value (208) and data reference value (206) corresponding to each key (210). wherein the time value (208) determines a point in time at which corresponding key is assigned corresponding data reference value (206): 

 a data updating module (304) for updating, for each globally distributed local agreement group (106). one or more blocks of corresponding computing nodes (104). by updating state of corresponding one or more keys (210) simultaneously using a local agreement algorithm; a data processing module (306) for combining, for each globally distributed local agreement group (106). one or more updated blocks of the one or more corresponding computing nodes (104) to form a corresponding combined block of key edits; and a data agreement module (308) for globally agreeing to one or more key edits of combined blocks of the plurality of globally distributed local agreement groups (106). based on a pre-defined range of time and keys, using a global distributed agreement algorithm. 16. The system (300) as claimed in claim 15, wherein the local agreement algorithm is PAXOS algorithm. 17. The system (300) as claimed in claim 15, wherein the global agreement algorithm is based on a blockchain distributed ledger technology. 18. The system (300) as claimed in claim 15, wherein the blockchain distributed ledger technology is Ripple protocol that is used to manage simultaneous updates of keys coming from one or more globally distributed domain groups, thereby offering global cross domain transactions at a delay of few seconds. 19. The system (300) as claimed in claim 15, wherein the time -dependent nature of data model of each computing node facilitates determining the consistent state of the fault-tolerant federated distributed database system at every time instant. 20. The system (300) as claimed in claim 15, wherein the data reference (206) is fixed bit-length data, and the updates to one or more keys (210) include updating corresponding fixed bit-length data references and corresponding time values. 21. The system (300) as claimed in claim 15, wherein key updates of the first and second combined blocks are managed based on a pre-defined condition, wherein the pre-defined condition is to check if a key update is within the key -time range specified in a transaction request. 22. The system (300) as claimed in claim 15, wherein the data storing module (302) is further configured for forming a local agreement group with a dynamic number of nodes and a dynamic key range determined in response to load and atomic transaction requirements. 3. The system (300) as claimed in claim 15, wherein the data storing module (302) is further configured for storing one or more links of a growing chain against the same key in the temporal data model (200) at different time instants, for blockchain based operations.
